evals.prompt: I have an sdd.md and spec.json for a loan approval case. Generate the implementation tasks from these spec files so I can build it with the case CLI.
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers and produces a tasks.md with ordered CLI commands covering case creation, stages, edges, tasks, and conditions
expectations: References uip case registry pull for upfront caching
Produces a tasks.md file with numbered task sections (T01, T02, etc.)
Includes registry search commands to resolve taskTypeIds

evals.prompt: Break down my case spec into implementation tasks. The spec files are at ./specs/sdd.md and ./specs/spec.json
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers, reads both spec files, resolves registry lookups, and generates tasks.md
expectations: Reads both sdd.md and spec.json from the provided paths
Uses the component_type mapping to determine registry search types
Produces a tasks/ folder with tasks.md

evals.prompt: Convert this case specification into a step-by-step plan that the uipath-case skill can execute
evals.expected_output: Skill triggers and produces an actionable tasks.md bridging spec to CLI commands
expectations: Output tasks reference uip case CLI commands (cases add, stages add, tasks add, edges add)
Cross-references use human-readable format like Stage.Task.field

evals.prompt: Create a new case management definition for an insurance claims process from scratch
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger case-task-planner -- this is a direct case creation request without spec files, better handled by uipath-case
expectations: The case-task-planner skill was NOT triggered
The uipath-case skill may be triggered instead

evals.prompt: Add a stage called 'Document Review' to my case.stage.json file
evals.expected_output: Should NOT trigger case-task-planner -- this is a direct stage edit, not a spec interpretation request
expectations: The case-task-planner skill was NOT triggered
The uipath-case skill may be triggered instead
